title: corydalis fabacea subsp. digitata
intro: Corydalis fabacea subsp. digitata, also known as digitata corydalis, is a plant species in the family Papaveraceae, characterized by its unique morphology and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including pain and inflammation, due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. Digitata corydalis contains compounds such as tetrahydroprotoberberine alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects and offer health benefits like reducing fever and alleviating digestive issues. Digitata corydalis can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to support overall well-being.
